title: Fix Daily Entry draft preview infinite render loop (React error #185 / maximum update depth)

requirements.summary: Stop the Daily Entry draft preview from entering an infinite render loop so the page renders normally and remains interactive.
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the draft preview and landing on the Daily Entry page does not throw React error #185.
The app does not display the root-route “Something went wrong” / error fallback when navigating to the Daily Entry page.
The Daily Entry page remains stable (no repeated re-render loop) while idle (no user interaction) for at least 30 seconds.
Restaurant Name select, Balance Date input, and ingredient Balance/Order inputs remain interactive and do not cause crashes when edited.
Save Record button remains clickable (subject to existing validation like required Restaurant Name and Balance Date) and does not trigger an infinite render loop.

file_operations.description: Fix the likely render-loop source by preventing the diagnostics subscription callback from calling setState when computed actor readiness/loading/error values are unchanged; ensure subscription lifecycle is stable and includes a one-time initial sync of diagnostics from the current query cache state.

Adjust Daily Entry to rely on the stabilized diagnostics state (and avoid any derived-state patterns that could amplify rapid diagnostics updates), ensuring the form, loading UI, retry UI, and save flow render without triggering repeated re-renders.

Add a regression check specific to the Daily Entry draft preview: open Daily Entry, confirm no generic error fallback appears, leave idle for 30 seconds, and verify key inputs remain editable without crashes.

requirements.summary: Harden the Daily Entry backend-readiness UI state so it only updates React state on meaningful actor readiness changes, preventing future maximum update depth regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend readiness/loading/error indicators on the Daily Entry page do not trigger continuous state updates when the underlying actor query state is unchanged.
Toggling backend actor states (loading → ready, ready → error via retry, etc.) updates the UI once per state change without creating a render loop.

file_operations.description: Implement a change-detection guard (e.g., compare next vs previous readiness/loading/error flags) so repeated react-query cache notifications do not continuously write the same diagnostics values to state; keep retry behavior intact while ensuring updates occur once per actual state change.

Verify the Daily Entry page’s readiness/loading/error UI uses the diagnostics flags in a way that does not introduce extra state writes (e.g., no derived setState loops) and that retry transitions update the UI a single time per change.
